fields.description:
# Emit canonical signed plan-material pins

## Goal
Bind every newly certified plan review to the narrow plan material of its direct children and direct prerequisites, without adding a ticket field or user-facing command.

## Implementation
- Extend the deterministic manifest so pin lines appear after optional verified-at-sha and before dep lines: plan-material-pin: <role> <canonical-ticket-id> <material-fingerprint>. Roles are exactly child and prerequisite; sort lexicographically by role (child before prerequisite), then lexicographically by canonical ID. A target in both roles has two pins; duplicate edges within one role collapse. Each fingerprint is the existing 16-lowercase-hex material_fingerprint(PlanContext) value for the target ticket, never a file dependency hash. Define ManifestFormatError in manifest.py; manifest_pins() returns [] only when pin lines are absent and raises it for any duplicate role+ID, unknown role, missing/extra token, invalid canonical ID, or non-16-hex fingerprint. Callers import the exception from manifest.py.
- Own an internal PlanRelationSnapshot contract containing subject_state, ticket_states_by_id, child_ids, prerequisite_ids, related_material, and ticket_store_revision. related_material is a deterministic tuple of {role, canonical_id, material_fingerprint} records sorted in manifest order and is the sole fingerprint carrier. Build the snapshot from one compiled ticket-state preload per review. Collect outgoing depends_on from the subject and inbound blocks by directly scanning relation entries in every preloaded state, including archived sources; do not call find_inbound_relationships because its active-source filter intentionally omits archived tickets. Resolve aliases and prefixes before deduplication; do not repeat store-wide reductions per target. The prerequisite criterion consumes this same object.
- Emit exactly one pin per canonical ID and role, in deterministic order. A ticket appearing in both supported roles receives one entry for each role; duplicate graph edges do not create duplicate entries.
- Run pin collection before prerequisite LLM evaluation. A target is readable only when its reference resolves uniquely to an existing reducer-readable state with matching canonical ID; archived and empty-description states qualify. Missing/deleted, ambiguous/malformed, reducer-error, canonical-ID-mismatch, or ticket-store-read failures raise internal PlanRelationSnapshotError with a closed reason code and optional target reference. The review boundary catches it before any LLM call, emits a structured ERROR log with reason and canonical/reference identity, and returns unsigned INDETERMINATE remediation through CLI and MCP; the error never produces a partial pin set or traceback fallback.
- Expose deterministic PlanRelationSnapshot collection plus ticket_store_revision, concretely the clean tickets tracker HEAD SHA read after ensure_fresh. The phase story brackets outside-lock collection with equal before/after revisions and verifies the same HEAD under the global lock before signature append+commit. The pin story does not acquire a separate sign lock.
- Always emit pins independently of project enforcement configuration. resign_plan_review collects the same PlanRelationSnapshot and passes its related_material to sign_plan_review, so re-signing cannot mint a new pinless attestation. Do not alter parent/child review prompts and do not add CLI/MCP operations.
- Preserve new-reader parsing of legacy manifests with no pins. Add a pre-feature-verifier fixture proving it authenticates the stored full DSSE manifest including unknown pin lines, then ignores only those lines for field interpretation; it must not reconstruct or rehash a filtered manifest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Passing reviews emit one deterministic pin for every direct child.
- [ ] Passing reviews emit one deterministic pin for every normalized prerequisite.
- [ ] Pins use authoritative canonical ticket IDs.
- [ ] Pin roles are exactly child or prerequisite.
- [ ] Pin fingerprints equal the target material_fingerprint(PlanContext) 16-hex value.
- [ ] Alias and short-ID references resolve before pin storage.
- [ ] Duplicate edges collapse within each role.
- [ ] Outgoing depends_on and inbound blocks produce the same prerequisite role.
- [ ] Archived inbound-block sources remain discoverable and readable.
- [ ] Empty-description targets remain readable.
- [ ] Every unreadable or unresolvable target returns unsigned INDETERMINATE before any LLM call.
- [ ] Each PlanRelationSnapshotError reason produces a structured ERROR log without a traceback fallback.
- [ ] PlanRelationSnapshot related_material contains the complete sorted role, canonical ID, and material fingerprint set.
- [ ] PlanRelationSnapshot carries the ticket-store revision used for bounded sign-time retry.
- [ ] Pin emission is unconditional across project configurations.
- [ ] Re-signing emits the same complete pin set as an ordinary new review.
- [ ] manifest_pins() returns [] only when pin lines are absent.
- [ ] Every malformed pin variant raises ManifestFormatError from manifest.py.
- [ ] New readers accept legacy unpinned manifests.
- [ ] A pre-feature reader authenticates the complete stored manifest, then ignores additive pin lines for field interpretation.
- [ ] No first-order ticket field is added for pins.
- [ ] No new CLI or MCP operation is introduced.

## Verification
- Run the recorded per-contract pytest commands for manifest parsing, relationship collection, re-sign parity, and legacy-reader compatibility.
- Failure-path tests cover every ManifestFormatError variant and every PlanRelationSnapshotError reason, and assert prerequisite LLM evaluation is never reached.
- Run make check and the non-integration test suite.
